memes.label: Aki of the King's Bodyguard
memes.description: A man of the King's bodyguard at Upsala, slain by Styrbiorn in a hall brawl after a drunken quarrel, whose killing stirred great discontent among the bonder-folk.

memes.label: Alaburg
memes.description: King Harald Bluetooth's royal stronghold on the Limfirth, where Styrbiorn's confrontation with the Danish king reached its climax.

memes.label: Concepts of the Saga
memes.description: Complete index of all concept entries in the Styrbiorn saga wiki

memes.description: Complete index of all event entries in the Styrbiorn saga wiki

memes.description: Complete index of all person entries in the Styrbiorn saga wiki

memes.label: Places of the Saga
memes.description: Complete index of all place entries in the Styrbiorn saga wiki

memes.label: Arland
memes.description: The southern Swedish estate of Skogul-Tosti, where King Eric the Victorious came a-guesting and first saw Sigrid, the woman who would become his Queen.

memes.label: Ball-Game at Kysingtarn
memes.description: A violent ball-game on the frozen Kysingtarn tarn near Upsala, where Styrbiorn's rivalry with Helgi and his brothers erupts into open physical conflict.

memes.label: Battle of Fyrisfield
memes.description: The climactic three-day battle on the water-meadows below Upsala where Styrbiorn the Strong led his Jomsviking host against King Eric the Victorious for the throne of Sweden (Chapter 15).

memes.label: Berserk-gang (Berserksgangr)
memes.description: A state of frenzied battle-rage in Norse warrior culture, described in the novel as a trance-like fury that overtakes fighters, causing them to bellow and fight with superhuman strength.

memes.label: Bessi Thorlakson
memes.description: A Jomsburg warrior in Styrbiorn's following, known for killing Eystein with an axe-hammer blow in a sea battle.

memes.label: Betrothal Feast of Styrbiorn and Thyri
memes.description: The great banquet in King Eric's hall at Upsala where Styrbiorn and Thyri were troth-plighted, the skald sang the Hell-ride of Brynhild, and the fateful gaze between Styrbiorn and Queen Sigrid was exchanged (Chapter 9).

memes.label: Biarmaland
memes.description: A northern land on the White Sea coast, raided by Styrbiorn and the Jomsvikings and known for its wealth and remoteness.

memes.label: Biorn the Broadwickers' Champion
memes.description: An Icelander of the Broadwickers' line, Styrbiorn's sworn foster-brother

memes.label: Biorn the Brisk
memes.description: A Jomsburg viking lord, named among the inner circle of Palnatoki's followers when Styrbiorn first arrived at the stronghold.

memes.label: Biorn the Old
memes.description: Father of Eric the Victorious and Olaf Biornson, grandfather of Styrbiorn — a Swedish king of the Yngling dynasty who left his two sons as joint kings.

memes.label: Blood-offering (Blót)
memes.description: The central Norse pagan sacrificial ritual (blót) in which blood-offerings were made to the gods, typically at seasonal feasts, as a communal act of worship and fidelity.

memes.label: Borgundholm
memes.description: Island home of Bui the Thick and Sigurd Cape, Jomsburg lords; modern Bornholm in the Baltic Sea.

memes.label: Bui the Stout
memes.description: A Jomsburg lord from Borgundholm, known as Bui the Stout (or Bui the Thick), a fierce and hot-headed warrior who taunted Styrbiorn at their first meeting and later became his most bloodthirsty ally in the campaign against Sweden.

memes.label: King Burisleif
memes.description: King of the Wends, originally betrothed to Thyri Haraldsdottir before Styrbiorn broke the bridal pact.

memes.label: Cowing of the Dane King
memes.description: Styrbiorn's political and military subjugation of King Harald Gormson of Denmark — a two-phase confrontation culminating in Harald's capitulation at Alaburg and his reduction to a subordinate ally.

memes.label: Danes
memes.description: The Danish people under King Harald Gormson — a major political and military faction in the novel, allied with Styrbiorn through his marriage to [[queen-thyri|Thyri]] Haraldsdottir.

memes.label: Danish Kingdom
memes.description: The realm of King Harald Gormson (Bluetooth), straddling the transition from the old Norse faith to Christianity — a kingdom divided between its pagan past and its Christian present.

memes.label: Earl Hakon of Norway
memes.description: The Earl of Norway who, after being forcibly baptised by Keisar Otto and King Harald Gormson, renounced Christianity by making a blood-offering and ruled Norway as an independent pagan lord.

memes.label: Earl Wolf
memes.description: King Eric's man, the brother of Styrbiorn's mother, who fostered the young prince after King Olaf's death and served as mediator between the stubborn uncle and headstrong nephew throughout the saga.

memes.label: East Gautland
memes.description: An eastern province of Sweden, listed among the lands owing allegiance to the Swedish crown (001-001).

memes.label: Einheriar
memes.description: The host of slain warriors chosen by Odin and the Valkyries to dwell in Valhalla, who fight each day and feast each night in preparation for Ragnarok.

memes.label: Estland
memes.description: Eastern Baltic land (modern Estonia), source of warriors and a target of Jomsburg raiding expeditions under Styrbiorn's leadership.

memes.label: Eystein the Fox
memes.description: A Norseman from Halogaland, companion of Styrbiorn in the East, famed for his skill with the axe — slain by Bessi Thorlakson during the ship-fight between Styrbiorn and Palnatoki at Jomsburg.

memes.label: Fetch (Fylgja)
memes.description: The fylgja (fetch) — a supernatural attendant spirit in Norse belief, often visible as an animal or woman to those with second sight, whose appearance foretells a person's fate or impending death.

memes.label: Fion (Funen)
memes.description: Large Danish island, home of Palnatoki and frequent waypoint for Styrbiorn and the Jomsvikings.

memes.label: Foster-brother (Fóstbróðir)
memes.description: A ritualised bond of sworn brotherhood in Norse culture, stronger than ordinary friendship and carrying mutual obligations of loyalty and protection; in the novel, the bond between Styrbiorn and Biorn Asbrandson is the defining personal relationship of the narrative.

memes.label: Fyrisfield
memes.description: The water-meadow by Fyriswater below Upsala where the final cataclysmic battle between Styrbiorn's host and King Eric's army took place (Chapter 15).

memes.label: Fyriswater
memes.description: The river that wound southward from beneath Upsala through the water-meadows, forming Fyrisfield — the stretch of land where the saga's climactic battle was fought.

memes.label: Garthrealm (Garðaríki)
memes.description: The Norse name for the realm of the Rus, encompassing the river-roads and trading towns of what would become Russia, under the overlordship of King Eric the Victorious.

memes.label: Gudrid
memes.description: Wife of Skogul-Tosti, mother of Sigrid (later Queen Sigrid), of the kin of the Earls of West Gautland — a proud, grasping, and politically calculating woman.

memes.label: Gunnstein Lowry
memes.description: A Jomsburg warrior of Styrbiorn's original Swedish following, who accompanied him on the expedition to reclaim the Swedish throne.

memes.label: Hall-feast (Norse Banquet Culture)
memes.description: The central social and political institution of Norse life — feasting in the King's hall with ale, meat, story-telling, and the ritual of cup-bearing — as depicted throughout the saga of Styrbiorn the Strong.

memes.label: Harald Bluetooth
memes.description: King of Denmark during Styrbiorn's time, a man of outward magnificence and inner frailty, forced into Christianity by Emperor Otto, who hosted Styrbiorn at Yule, quarrelled with him over his daughter [[queen-thyri|Thyri]], and was ultimately cowed into submission at Alaburg.

memes.label: Harald the Grenlander
memes.description: A former suitor of Sigrid who she rejected; later became a king (003-014).

memes.label: Helgi Ericson
memes.description: Helgi Ericson, one of King Eric's three illegitimate sons, captain of the King's bodyguard at the Battle of Fyrisfield.

memes.label: Helsingland
memes.description: A northern Swedish province beyond which lay the Finn-heaths, whose earl mustered troops for King Eric at the battle of Fyrisfield.

memes.label: Heming
memes.description: The second son of Earl Strut-Harald of Skaney, a Jomsburg lord and captain who commanded the left wing at the Battle of Fyrisfield.

memes.label: Hlymdale
memes.description: A legendary location from the Nibelung cycle, dwelling of Heimir and Brynhild, referenced in verse during the saga.

memes.label: Holmgarth
memes.description: The great Varangian trading city of the east — Novgorod in modern Russia — where Styrbiorn sought refuge during his exile, found a powerful host, and received the golden treasures of a princess, and from which Valdimar the Prince's son marched to his death at Fyrisfield.

memes.label: Howe (Burial Mound)
memes.description: A burial mound or barrow of the Norse tradition, serving as the final resting place of kings and warriors in the saga of Styrbiorn the Strong.

memes.label: Ingibiorg
memes.description: Queen of Sweden, wife of King Olaf Biornson, mother of Styrbiorn the Strong, daughter of Earl Thrand of Sula.

memes.label: Jarnberaland
memes.description: A northern province of Sweden, one of the lands that mustered troops for the Battle of Fyrisfield.

memes.label: Jomsburg
memes.description: The legendary fortress-stronghold of the Jomsvikings on the Wendland coast, built by Palnatoki with stone walls, a sea-gate, and a harbour capable of sheltering three hundred long-ships — the brotherhood's impenetrable home and seat of power.

memes.label: Jomsburg Law (Jomsviking Warrior Code)
memes.description: The sworn warrior code governing the Jomsviking brotherhood at Jomsburg — a binding covenant of age limits, mutual vengeance, equal plunder-sharing, and absolute courage that defined the elite fellowship Palnatoki captained and Styrbiorn joined.

memes.label: Jomsvikings
memes.description: The elite warrior brotherhood of Jomsburg, bound by their sworn law under Palnatoki's captaincy, feared as the greatest champions of the North whose summer harrying brought "good fee and good renown."

memes.label: Jutland
memes.description: The mainland peninsula of Denmark, bordered by the Jutland sea and the Limfirth, home to King Harald's strongholds and the staging ground for Danish naval power.

memes.label: Eric the Victorious
memes.description: King of Sweden at the time of the saga, uncle of Styrbiorn the Strong, who faces his nephew's challenge for the throne and defends his kingdom at the Battle of Fyrisfield

memes.label: King Eric's Bodyguard
memes.description: The elite household warriors of King Eric the Victorious — a personal retinue that serves as the King's last line of defense at the Battle of Fyrisfield.

memes.label: Kinship Networks of the Saga
memes.description: "An analysis of the four major kin-groups in Styrbiorn the Strong — Yngling dynasty, Skogul-Tosti's house, Strut-Harald's Skaney network, and Harald Bluetooth's Danish house — and how their intersections drive the saga's political conflicts."

memes.label: Kirialaland
memes.description: A distant eastern land of mystery and ancient worship, home to a great temple amid the wolds where Styrbiorn's companion Bui sought strange adventures.

memes.label: Kysingtarn
memes.description: A tarn (small lake) in Sweden where a great ball-game gathering was held, leading to the confrontation between Styrbiorn and Helgi Ericson that precipitated Styrbiorn's exile.

memes.label: Lambi the White
memes.description: A bonder from Stocksound in Tenthland who was proclaimed king by the bonders in defiance of Styrbiorn, later outlawed by King Eric and slain by Styrbiorn off the coast of Skaney.

memes.label: Limfirth (Limfjord)
memes.description: The great firth in northern Jutland, strategic waterway that served as the approach to King Harald's stronghold at Alaburg.

memes.label: Long-ship
memes.description: The long-ship (langskip) was the primary warship of the Norse peoples, a sleek, shallow-draft vessel propelled by oars and sail, central to Viking identity, raiding, trade, and the projection of power across the seas.

memes.label: Moldi
memes.description: Styrbiorn's pet musk-ox from the northlands beyond Helsingland — his wrestling companion and a living symbol of his wild, untamed nature.

memes.label: Norse Fate (Wyrd/Doom)
memes.description: The Norse concept of fate (wyrd, "weird") — the belief that destiny is predetermined by the Norns (the three Fates) who weave the web of events, and that no man may avoid his ordained doom.

memes.label: Oath (Eiðr)
memes.description: A sacred binding vow in Norse culture, sworn on the gods or on holy objects; the Jomsborg fellowship was founded upon strong oaths sworn to Thor, and oath-breaking carried the penalty of death.

memes.label: Odin (the All-Father)
memes.description: The chief god of the Norse pantheon, called All-Father, God of Hosts, and Whisperer in the Wind in the novel; presides over Valhalla and receives the battle-slain brought by the Valkyries.

memes.label: Olaf Biornson
memes.description: Joint King of Sweden with his brother Eric, father of Styrbiorn Olafson. Died before the events of the novel.

memes.label: Outlawry (Skógarmaðr)
memes.description: The Norse legal penalty of outlawry — banishment from society, loss of all legal protection, and the status of being a "forest-man" (skógarmaðr) whom any man might kill without consequence.

memes.label: Palnatoki
memes.description: The legendary founder and captain of Jomsburg who tested Styrbiorn in single combat, admitted him into the Jomsviking fellowship, and later pledged the full support of Jomsburg to his cause.

memes.label: Sigrid the Haughty
memes.description: Queen of Sweden, wife of King Eric the Victorious, who first meets Styrbiorn

memes.label: Thyri Haraldsdottir
memes.description: Daughter of King Harald Gormson of Denmark, betrothed to Styrbiorn, who

memes.label: Ring-byrny (Mail Armour)
memes.description: A byrny (Old Norse brynja) is a shirt of interwoven iron rings worn as body armour by Norse warriors, a mark of wealth, status, and martial identity in the Viking Age.

memes.label: Roiskeld
memes.description: Royal seat of King Harald Gormson in Sealand, Denmark, where Styrbiorn spent the winter during his Yuletide visit.

memes.label: Scat (Skattr)
memes.description: The Norse system of tribute or tax (Old Norse "skattr") paid by a subordinate ruler to an overlord, signifying political subordination; the refusal to pay scat is a declaration of independence.

memes.label: Sea-Battle at Jomsburg
memes.description: The ship-to-ship sea-fight and subsequent single combat between Styrbiorn the Strong and Palnatoki off the walls of Jomsburg, which won Styrbiorn admission to the Jomsviking brotherhood (Chapter 4).

memes.label: Sealand (Zealand)
memes.description: The largest Danish island, site of King Harald's great house at Roiskeld and the narrow seas between Sealand and Skaney.

memes.label: Sigtun
memes.description: The port town on the Swedish coast near Upsala where Styrbiorn landed upon his return from exile, and where King Eric rode down to greet him with a great company.

memes.label: Sigurd Cape
memes.description: A Jomsburg lord from Borgundholm, brother of Bui the Thick, who fought on the right wing at the Battle of Fyrisfield.

memes.label: Sigvaldi Jarl
memes.description: Son of Earl Strut-Harald of Skaney, a Jomsburg lord known for his cunning, cautious diplomacy, and reluctance to support Styrbiorn's campaign against Sweden.

memes.label: Skaldic Lore (Norse Poetry)
memes.description: The ancient Norse tradition of skaldic poetry — the art of the skalds, the poets and storytellers who preserved history, religion, and fame through oral verse in the Viking world, as depicted throughout the saga of Styrbiorn the Strong.

memes.label: Skaney (Skane)
memes.description: The Danish earldom of Strut-Harald, lying east across the Sound from Sealand — a strategic coastal territory that served as a waypoint for Styrbiorn's fleet and a seat of semi-regal authority in the saga's political geography.

memes.label: Skogul-Tosti
memes.description: A mighty nobleman of Sweden from Arland, father of Queen Sigrid the Haughty, who hosted feasts for King Eric and brought his men to the King's aid at the Battle of Fyrisfield.

memes.label: Slaying of Aki
memes.description: The killing of Aki of the King's Bodyguard by Styrbiorn at a feast in Upsala — the inciting event that sets Styrbiorn's exile in motion.

memes.label: Spae-Wife's Lay (Völuspá)
memes.description: The Spae-Wife's Lay (Völuspá) — the great Norse creation-and-doom poem from the Poetic Edda, recited by Biorn in King Harald's hall at Yule, foretelling the beginning and end of all things and the ways of the Gods with men.

memes.label: Stocksound
memes.description: A settlement in Tenthland, Sweden — home of Lambi the White who was proclaimed king by the bonders in defiance of Styrbiorn.

memes.label: Strut-Harald the Earl
memes.description: A famous Earl of Skaney in the Danish realm, father of Sigvaldi, Heming, and Thorkell the High, who lived in near-regal magnificence.

memes.label: Styrbiorn the Strong
memes.description: The protagonist of the saga, son of King Olaf, raised after his father's

memes.label: Styrbiorn the Strong
memes.description: A 1926 novel by E. R. Eddison retelling the Old Norse saga of the exiled Swedish prince Styrbiorn, who challenged his uncle King Eric the Victorious for the throne of Sweden. (P02-001)

memes.label: Styrbiorn's Journey
memes.description: "A geographic and thematic analysis of Styrbiorn's exile arc: a ring from Uppsala through Holmgarth, Jomsburg, and Denmark, returning to Sweden — a physical journey that mirrors his transformation from exiled prince to tragic hero."

memes.label: Swedish Kingdom
memes.description: The realm of the Swedes under King Eric the Victorious, ruling from Upsala over the Up-Swedes, the folk of Nether-realm and Southmanland, East and West Gautland, and all lands eastaway from the Elf to the main sea.

memes.label: Tenthland
memes.description: A Swedish province; home of Lambi the White, who was proclaimed rival king in Styrbiorn's despite.

memes.label: The Thing Assembly
memes.description: The lawful assembly of the Swedish freemen at the Thing-stead in Upsala, where kings were proclaimed, laws were spoken, and the voice of the bonders could make or unmake the realm's rulers.

memes.label: The Thing at Upsala
memes.description: The lawful assembly summoned by King Eric at Upsala to grant Styrbiorn half the kingdom, which never came to pass — the great constitutional moment that was promised but delayed and ultimately overtaken by events (Chapter 9).

memes.label: Thing-stead
memes.description: The sacred assembly ground in Norse society where free men gathered to settle disputes, make laws, and proclaim kings

memes.label: Thora Olafsdottir
memes.description: Eldest daughter of King Olaf Biornson and Queen Ingibiorg, sister of Styrbiorn the Strong, along with her sisters Thurid, Asdis, and Aud.

memes.label: Thorgisl
memes.description: One of King Eric's three bastard sons, a courtier and antagonist to Styrbiorn Olafson.

memes.label: Thorgnyr the Lawman
memes.description: The aged lawman and trusted counsellor to King Eric of Sweden, whose wisdom and steadfast loyalty guided the King through the crisis of Styrbiorn's rebellion.

memes.label: Thorir
memes.description: One of King Eric's three bastard sons, a cynical courtier who coined the mocking epithet "lap-king" for Styrbiorn.

memes.label: Thorkell the High
memes.description: The third son of Earl Strut-Harald of Skaney, a Jomsburg lord and warrior who commanded the left wing at the Battle of Fyrisfield.

memes.label: Thrand of Sula
memes.description: Earl of Sula, father of Queen Ingibiorg, and maternal grandfather of Styrbiorn the Strong.

memes.label: Thyri Haraldsdottir
memes.description: Daughter of King Harald Bluetooth of Denmark, betrothed and later wed to Styrbiorn the Strong — a princess of few words, keen perception, and steadfast devotion who loved Styrbiorn deeply.

memes.label: Timeline of the Saga
memes.description: "A chronological ordering of the major events in Styrbiorn the Strong, spanning approximately 25 years from Styrbiorn's childhood through his death at Fyrisfield and reception into Valhalla."

memes.label: Tofa Strut-Haraldsdottir
memes.description: Daughter of Strut-Harald the Earl of Skaney, wife of Sigurd Cape of Borgundholm, and sister of Sigvaldi, Heming, and Thorkell the High — part of the powerful Strut-Harald kin-network in Jomsburg.

memes.label: Upsala
memes.description: The royal seat of the Swedish kings, home to King Eric the Victorious, site of the great temple, the Thing-stead, the King's hall, and King Olaf's howe — the heart of the Swede-realm.

memes.label: Vagn Akison
memes.description: The youngest Jomsviking ever admitted to the brotherhood — the only man besides Styrbiorn to be taken into Jomsburg under the minimum age of eighteen winters.

memes.label: Valhalla (Valhöll)
memes.description: Odin's hall in Asgard where warriors who die in battle are taken by the Valkyries to feast and fight until Ragnarok.

memes.label: Valkyries
memes.description: The Valkyries (Old Norse Valkyrjur, "choosers of the slain") are Odin's divine shield-maidens who select warriors destined to die in battle and conduct them to Valhalla, where they serve the Einheriar at the eternal feast.

memes.label: War-arrow (Herör)
memes.description: A token of mobilisation in Norse warfare — an arrow sent from district to district to summon the levy (leding) for war; in the novel, King Eric shears up the war-arrow to call the all-folk hosting before the battle of Fyrisfield.

memes.label: Wendland
memes.description: The kingdom of the Wends on the Baltic coast, ruled by King Burisleif, whose daughter Thyri was married to King Harald Bluetooth.

memes.label: West Gautland
memes.description: A western province of Sweden under King Eric's dominion; homeland of Queen Sigrid's maternal kin and source of warriors in Styrbiorn's host.

memes.label: Windbergsfell
memes.description: The mountain east of Upsala with its scree-slopes and red cliffs, which flanked the valley where the Battle of Fyrisfield was fought and from whose crest the Queen watched the final day's fighting.

memes.label: Yngling-kin (Divine Lineage)
memes.description: The legendary dynasty of Swedish kings who traced their lineage to the god Yngvi-Frey (Freyr), whose blood runs in the veins of King Eric and Styrbiorn, legitimizing their rule through divine descent.

memes.label: Yule Feast in Denmark
memes.description: The great Yule feast held by King Harald Gormson at his hall in Roiskeld, Sealand, where Styrbiorn and Biorn guested, Biorn recited the Spae-Wife's Lay, and the seeds of future conflict were sown between Styrbiorn and the Dane-King (Chapter 5).
